title: shorea rugosa var. uliginosa
intro: Shorea rugosa var. uliginosa, also known as the Swamp Shorea, is a variant of the Shorea rugosa tree species native to Southeast Asia. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating various respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Shorea rugosa var. uliginosa include reducing inflammation, alleviating symptoms of respiratory diseases, and acting as an antioxidant, which helps protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. In herbal preparations, the plant's medicinal properties are often extracted through steam distillation or solvent extraction, and the resulting essential oils or extracts are used in traditional medicine, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
